```{=html}
<aside class="side-panel glossary-sidebar">
<details open>
<summary>Glossary</summary>
<dl>
  <dt>Fee book</dt>
  <dd>Quote <code>(1 + f) · P · S<sub>t</sub></code>, remit the
  protocol price, keep the markup <code>f · P · S<sub>t</sub></code>.
  Revenue <code>≥ 0</code> by construction; no inventory.</dd>

  <dt>Principal book</dt>
  <dd>Quote a fixed <code>Q</code> up front, source the tokens at
  spot or pre-buy. Five variants below.</dd>

  <dt>Matched</dt>
  <dd>Pre-buy all inventory at <code>t = 0</code>. Terminal P&amp;L
  deterministic.</dd>

  <dt>Back-to-back</dt>
  <dd>Source every tonne at spot. Short kVCM beta over
  <code>[0, T]</code>; upside capped, downside unbounded.</dd>

  <dt>Partial</dt>
  <dd>Pre-buy a fraction <code>α</code> of inventory at
  <code>t = 0</code>; the rest back-to-back. Mean interpolates
  linearly, SD collapses as <code>(1 − α)</code>.</dd>

  <dt>Syndicated</dt>
  <dd>Cede a fraction <code>β</code> of the stochastic leg to a
  counterparty against an up-front premium. Variance collapses as
  <code>(1 − α)²(1 − β)²</code>.</dd>

  <dt>Switching</dt>
  <dd>Flip pricing from <code>Q</code> to a fee book when
  <code>S<sub>t</sub></code> first crosses an upper barrier
  <code>h · S<sub>0</sub></code>. Truncates the loss tail rather
  than rescaling it.</dd>

  <dt>Coverage α</dt>
  <dd>Fraction of horizon served from pre-bought inventory. The
  linear dial on tail risk for the principal book.</dd>

  <dt><code>Q*</code></dt>
  <dd>The fixed quote at which the fee and back-to-back books share
  expected revenue. Matching means does <em>not</em> match
  distributions.</dd>
</dl>
</details>
</aside>
```
